channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Visa Infinite Cardholders Can Claim Free Regal Movie Tickets and Popcorn
item.description: Visa Infinite cardholders can take advantage of a promotional offer through the Visa Infinite Concierge service to receive two free Regal movie tickets and popcorn. To qualify, cardholders must regist...

item.title: Comprehensive Review of the Chase Ink Business Cash Credit Card
item.description: The Chase Ink Business Cash Credit Card is a no-annual-fee business card that stands out for its high-value rewards and versatility. It offers 5X Chase Ultimate Rewards points on spending at cell phon...

item.title: Czech Designer Reimagines City Logos in Retro 1980s Style
item.description: Czech designer Kostya Petrenko has created a series of city logos inspired by the distinctive aesthetic of the 1980s. The project applies the era's characteristic slightly pixelated, analog glow to we...

item.title: Capital One Shopping Offers High Cash Back Rates on Travel and Retail Purchases
item.description: Capital One Shopping, a public shopping portal that does not require a Capital One card, has recently rolled out a series of elevated cash back offers. Users have reported receiving targeted email pro...

item.title: US Bank Altitude Reserve May Be Product-Changed With Potential Annual Fee Refund
item.description: The US Bank Altitude Reserve card, once highly regarded for its rewards, experienced significant changes in December 2025 that reduced its overall appeal. Previously, the card offered 3X points on mob...

item.title: Maya Hawke and Christian Lee Hutson Wed in Intimate Valentine’s Day Ceremony in NYC
item.description: Actor and musician Maya Hawke and singer-songwriter Christian Lee Hutson have officially married, celebrating their union on Valentine’s Day at St. George’s Episcopal Church in New York City. The wedd...

item.title: Spirit Airlines Introduces Free Spirit Debit Card with Monthly Fee and Limited Rewards
item.description: Spirit Airlines is preparing to launch the Free Spirit Debit Card, with a landing page now live outlining most of its key features, although applications are not yet open. The card will carry a monthl...

item.title: Guide to Choosing Additive-Free Electrolytes for Daily Hydration
item.description: Electrolytes are no longer just for athletes—they are increasingly used by people in daily routines, such as during long workdays, travel, or hot weather. As usage becomes frequent, many individuals a...

item.title: An Analysis of Imperialism as a Global System of Economic, Military, and Political Control
item.description: The article argues that imperialism constitutes a global system of domination rooted primarily in economic power, drawing heavily on Lenin’s theory in “Imperialism, the Highest Stage of Capitalism.” I...

item.title: China Leads the World in Wind Power Production Despite Trump’s Claims
item.description: During remarks at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, President Donald Trump criticized wind energy and claimed that China does not use the wind turbines it manufactures. Speaking to an au...

item.title: United TravelBank Purchases May No Longer Qualify for Airline Incidental Fee Credits on Cards Like Amex Platinum
item.description: Recent reports, primarily from Doctor of Credit and user discussions on Reddit and Flyertalk, suggest that deposits to United Airlines' TravelBank no longer trigger airline incidental fee reimbursemen...

item.title: An Analysis of Michael Abrash’s Assembly Optimizations in Quake’s Software Renderer
item.description: The article examines whether Michael Abrash’s hand-written x86 assembly code truly doubled the performance of Quake’s software renderer, as suggested by John Carmack’s original readme after the 1999 s...

item.title: ‘Wuthering Heights’ achieves strong global box office debut despite mixed reviews
item.description: Emerald Fennell’s reinterpretation of Emily Brontë’s classic novel 'Wuthering Heights' has launched to impressive box office success, earning US$76.8 million in its opening weekend, making it the bigg...

item.title: Two Exceptionally Rare Roman Mithraic Altars from Scotland to be Exhibited
item.description: Two exceptionally rare Roman Mithraic altars discovered in 2010 at Inveresk, East Lothian, Scotland, are being displayed publicly for the first time at the National Museum of Scotland in Edinburgh. Th...

item.title: LAYER and MAZZU Introduce Modular Spring-Based Camping Mattress Without Foam
item.description: Design studio LAYER has partnered with MAZZU to rethink one of camping’s most familiar pieces of gear: the sleeping mattress. Rather than relying on traditional foam and air-filled structures, which o...

item.title: Democratizing the Economy to Address Climate and Social Crises
item.description: The article argues that the current capitalist system is inherently incapable of addressing the pressing social and ecological crises of the 21st century. Capitalism prioritizes profit maximization ov...

item.title: Measles outbreak infects over 60 children in Enfield amid low vaccination rates
item.description: A measles outbreak has affected more than 60 children in north London, particularly in the Enfield area, where cases have been reported across seven schools and a nursery. Several children required ho...

item.title: Bianca Censori Named as Witness in Kanye West’s Legal Case Over Former Employee Claims
item.description: Bianca Censori has been called as a potential witness in her husband Kanye West’s ongoing legal dispute with his former project manager, Tony Saxon. Saxon’s lawsuit alleges unsafe and unethical workin...

item.title: Former Trump Supporter Expresses Disillusionment Over Political Corruption and Child Abuse Allegations
item.description: A former supporter of President Donald Trump, Blake Humphreys, has gone viral on Facebook after posting a detailed and emotional message criticizing the president for failing to address corruption and...

item.title: Thousands of Immigrants Detained Illegally by ICE in First Five Months of Trump’s Term
item.description: Between October of the previous year and February 2026, U.S. immigration authorities (ICE) have been found to have illegally detained over 4,400 immigrants, according to federal court rulings. In many...
